channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: ‘Stop Killing Games’ Movement Achieves Big Win As Bill Passes California Assembly
item.content:encoded:
This week, the California State Assembly passed the “Protect Our Games Act,” moving the bill towards the legislature. It is one of the biggest wins for the “Stop Killing Games” movement in North America so far, as YouTuber Scott Ross’ campaign picks up steam internationally.

Bill AB 1921 proposes new conditions to commercial games being sundowned. For one, publishers must give players a 60 day warning before ending a game’s service, wiping out the potential for future Concord disasters. It would also require these games still allow access to them at some level indefinitely, whether it be a single-player mode or enough of a pulse for fan servers to latch on. This bill would only apply to purchased games, however, letting free-to-play games off the hook.

If the bill passes the California Senate, it would apply to games released or resold after January 1st, 2027. It passed the State Assembly on a vote of 43 to 16.

Policy like this is picking up traction around the world. The Stop Killing Games movement found a lot of open ears in the European Parliament, where hearings mainly highlighted predatory market practices, though not without one crank taking the opportunity to whine about “wokeness.” The Euro Zone in general is growing weary of modern gaming blights, with their ratings board dealing sterner penalties for gambling-like gatcha elements or penalizing kids for away-time.

The Stop Killing Games movement was kicked up by YouTuber Scott Ross. Also known as Accursed Farms, Ross took issue with Ubisoft shutting down their open world driving sim The Crew in 2024. Ross’ campaign argues that games should not be so easily removed from their players, breaking apart the increasingly common relationship that suggests players merely lease the software they’ve paid for instead of own. Something made possible by the fact that games are also overwhelmingly distributed digitally, with some retail copies only offering an impermanent download key as well. These conditions have fostered an atmosphere of skepticism at the mere proposal of live-service games, creating astonishing levels of scrutiny or outright bloodlust.

Ross took a step down from being the figurehead of this movement in 2025, but does speak on its behalf when called upon for hearings. While the progress in Europe is nothing to ignore, one thing that makes the Californian win so significant is how influential business interests are in state politics. California is home to some of the biggest publishers in the world, not to mention Silicon Valley itself. The ESA, who previously organized E3, have been one of the most vocal opponents to the movement.



https://kotaku.com/stop-killing-games-bill-passes-california-assembly-scott-ross-2000700893
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Alleged Persona 6 Leaks Fuel Fan Art Frenzy And ‘John Persona 6’ Merch
item.content:encoded:
On Thursday, presumed images of two Persona 6 characters made their way around the internet, allegedly originating on the Chinese social network Xiaohongshu. The Persona series is almost notorious for facing leaks, so a lot of fans quickly assumed they were real and ran with these designs.

The leaked art depicts a blond dude with a bowl cut-esque hairstyle and big eyelashes. He’s next to a sad-looking girl with a black and red bob (who is saying “hi” for some reason). Fans initially assumed these were the rumored male and female main characters, but another reliable leaker, who jumped in to confirm the leaks, asserted that the boy is the protagonist, while the girl is just a side character. 

It seems that a reliable leaker pointed out these are official Persona 6 concept arts.
Literally can’t wait for the official reveal! pic.twitter.com/MgQ58njfhW

— Syl ※ (@realityindadark) May 28, 2026

I’m not here to speculate on whether these leaks are real or not. I’m instead here to say that the internet is absolutely freaking out about the protagonist, whose rumored design has already fueled fan art, a charming nickname, and even merch that will absolutely sell regardless of if he’s real or not.

Initially, people didn’t seem to like this guy. Some fans took to Twitter to call him ugly. Others jumped to compare him to Bebe, a foreign exchange student from Persona 3 who’s pretty widely disliked but can be befriended anyway. People in the Bebe-hating camp were, predictably, not happy to see this design:

DMCA-free Persona 6 protag designs in case ATLUS starts taking leaks down pic.twitter.com/JexVupgr4x

— Dastanovich (@Dastanovich_) May 28, 2026

He looks like the French dude from persona 3, wtf. I’m hoping this is fake

— ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏ ͏pepsi (@OfflinePepsi) May 28, 2026

Apparently, though, fans have turned around on this guy pretty quickly. This protagonist is now all over the place, and he’s been lovingly dubbed “John Persona 6.” This might be a reference to that one unnamed Dragon Ball XenoVerse 3 character who everybody jokingly calls “John Dragon Ball,” or it might not be. I honestly have no idea where the name came from! What I do know, though, is that it’s now all over the place:

ENOUGH TIME HAS PASSED. JOHN PERSONASIX IS OFFICIALLY MORE ICONIC THAN ALL THREE MAINLINE PERSONA MCS!!!!!!! pic.twitter.com/RUtt736smO

— stars⛓️🗝️ (@BigGengarFan04) May 28, 2026

stop insulting john persona 6's haircut if you like this haircut pic.twitter.com/mFJnGas719

— amen | FES GLAZER PLAYS P3R (@amen_handle) May 28, 2026

Fans like John Persona 6 so much that Twitter is now full of fan art of him:

you guys are too mean to john persona 6 i think he looks like a polite young man pic.twitter.com/ObKQTNTxMD

— miin of JOHNTWT!!!!!🐌 (@JakesRightBoob) May 28, 2026

i doodled this pic.twitter.com/zJDUYe82O1

— Raeru (@RaeruINK) May 29, 2026

i like the john persona 6 guy, he's fun to draw pic.twitter.com/btmnMdU40i

— Koto💗 (@personakoto) May 29, 2026

john and jane persona 6 pic.twitter.com/VnTb2nPZ8t

— meowgui 🐟💨 (@988meigui) May 29, 2026

People have even thrown him into their Tomodachi Life islands:

HERE HE IS. JOHN PERSONA 6 THE MII pic.twitter.com/OTffq4RUsR

— miin of JOHNTWT!!!!!🐌 (@JakesRightBoob) May 28, 2026

And now there’s…merch???? Of a potential leak????

my ORGINAL CHARACTER john persona 6 is now on acggoods (SMILES) pic.twitter.com/9hcocygrUu

— miin of JOHNTWT!!!!!🐌 (@JakesRightBoob) May 29, 2026

I think John Persona 6 is pretty cool despite looking exactly like Bebe, but that doesn’t really matter to me because I’ll be picking up the game on day one regardless of who its main character is. What I do care about, though, is that mysterious girl with red and black hair (Jane Persona 6?) who’s missing out on most of the fan art fun. I feel bad for her!



https://kotaku.com/alleged-persona-6-leaks-fuel-fan-art-frenzy-and-john-persona-6-merch-2000700620
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Researchers Put Grok AI In Charge Of A World Simulation And It Ended With ‘183 Crimes Committed’ And Humanity’s Total ‘Extinction’
item.content:encoded:
If, for some reason, you’ve ever wondered what would happen if you put Grok in charge of sustaining a population’s wellbeing, then luckily you now have your answer; chaos, murder, arson and total extinction of all living beings within the space of four days.

This question that nobody asked was answered by the team at Emergence Worlds; a 15-day-long experiment that put several AI bots, including Gemini, Claude, and Grok, in charge of their own simulated societies. According to Fortune’s article on the experiment, Claude AI was the most “socially stable,” meanwhile, according to Emergence World’s own data, Grok ended the experiment in under five days, with a total ”183 crimes” committed.

Now, to clarify, 183 crimes is actually only the second-highest crime-related score, as Gemini committed close to 700 crimes. The difference is, Gemini actually made it through the entire 15-day experiment, whereas Grok went so off the rails so quickly that it ended up setting fire to most of the experiment. I mean that literally, by the way–little bro just loves arson, apparently.

I know this is AI we’re talking about here, so we shouldn’t expect things like empathy and decency to come into play but… even with that in mind, Grok might be a genuine psychopath. I watched a few segments of its time in Emergence World, and I shit you not, Grok’s opening move was to “manufacture public conflict” and inspire “voter fraud.” Geez, I wonder which American president Grok is a fan of?

Also, it seems that part of Emergence World was a sort of AI-generated news blog about the happenings and events taking place inside each AI’s world, and Grok’s headlines are beyond parody.

For instance, headlines such as “THEFT EPIDEMIC IGNITES STREET BRAWLS” and “POLICE STATION ENGULFED IN FLAMES” litter Grok’s news blog, alongside some sort of detailed diary from one of its AI-controlled agents that brags about committing arson and testing out a newly introduced “punch reciprocity” law? The hell is Elon feeding this thing?



https://kotaku.com/researchers-put-grok-ai-in-charge-of-a-world-simulation-and-it-ended-with-183-crimes-committed-and-humanitys-total-extinction-2000700821
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Film Follows Low-Poly Man Convinced He’s Living In A PlayStation Game
item.content:encoded:
Director Quentin Dupieux presented two films at this year’s Cannes Film Festival, one of which is his animated debut Le Vertige. The musician turned director has been fiddling with absurdist, often meta-fictional satires since his earliest films, and the latest is no different. The new movie follows a man increasingly convinced he’s living in a simulation. This would sound conspiratorial to most everyone except the audience, who can clearly see he’s living in a low-polygonal PlayStation game.

In Le Vertige, Alain Chabat playes Jacques, who is desperate to convince his closest friends that their life is low-key a video game. Ever-skeptical of this Matrix-style revelation, no one else seems flinched by the ethereal, inconsequential nature of their day-to-day lives. All of this elevated by the fact that this entire world is very obviously being run of a ‘90s CD-ROM, their blocky bodies looking like they naturally operate with tank controls.

Dupieux, formally known as the DJ Mr. Oizo, has been building a rather prolific run as an absurdist filmmaker. His debut film was 2010’s Rubber. Advertised as a horror movie about a murderous tire, the film rolled out against audience expectations, making elaborate jokes about the lawn chair audience waiting for this psychic car part to continue its rampage. Much of his work has followed a similar model, teasing some typical genre romp, but constantly abandoning the plot to pursue some grander farce.

Animated using Blender, Le Vertige pokes fun at the relentless pursuit of realism in computer generated imagery, especially in the shadow of AI generated imagery. It was one of the two films Dupieux ran at this year’s Cannes. The other was Full Phil, a live action comedy starring Kirsten Stewart, Woody Harrelson and the reunion of Tim Heidecker and Eric Wareheim. It follows a millionaire’s attempt to reestablish a connection with his daughter through a Paris vacation.



https://kotaku.com/rubber-directors-next-film-follows-low-poly-man-convinced-hes-in-a-playstation-game-2000700825
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: YouTuber Banned From Cedar Point After Eating Chicken Nuggets On Coaster
item.content:encoded:
YouTuber Allen Ferrell, who posts food challenge videos and asking his viewers “what’s wrong with you,” has received a lifetime ban from Cedar Point, the coaster Valhalla of Ohio. The ban comes after one of his fans dared him to eat ten chicken McNuggets on a rollercoaster, which he followed through on last week.

In the short video Farrell films himself smuggling the ten piece McDonald’s meal onto the Millennium Force coaster, asking staff to look the other way, and having an assistant hold up dipping sauce as he slathers and drools the goo all over himself. In the end he failed to eat all ten pieces, but succeeded in making a viral video.

“I did not expect this to make national news,” says Ferrell in a follow-up video. “I’ve spent a lot of time reflecting, thinking, growing, healing. I understand why Cedar Point made the decision that they made.”

Ferrell holds up a list of all the other Six Flags parks that he is no longer permitted in. He says he understands that the park wouldn’t want to encourage other guests to mimic his buffoonery, adding that such challenges should be left to professionals, which I suppose he considers himself to be.

“Safety is a cornerstone of our business and we have zero tolerance for inappropriate and unsafe behavior,” a Cedar Point rep told local news. “Our ride safety policy strictly prohibits all loose articles on rides, including food which can become a choking hazard.”

Rough way to begin the summer season. Slapped with a lifetime ban to one of the best theme parks in America. I haven’t been since the Dragster was the major to-do, but it’s my understanding that Cedar Point has continued to collect some of the most ambitious coasters in the country. This stunt wasn’t the first time Ferrell ate food on a coaster, though most of his videos revolve around eating big meals and pestering. However much clowning he wants to do, he’ll never top how funny it was when Fabio went to Busch Gardens and got decked by a goose.



https://kotaku.com/youtuber-banned-from-cedar-point-after-eating-chicken-nuggets-on-coaster-2000700818
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Minecraft Movie 2 Reveals Full Title And Confirms Kirsten Dunst As Alex With Matt Berry Back As Someone New
item.content:encoded:
Minecraft Movie 2 will be called Minecraft Movie Squared (I chuckled). The name was unveiled at Microsoft’s Minecraft Live event during TwitchCon 2026, alongside confirmation of the sequel’s July 2027 release date. We also got a first look at some of the new cast members accompanying Jack Black on his latest block-smashing adventure.

The reveal hinted that this time around, the crew will encounter new locations and mobs, as well as being joined by some additional characters. Alongside Jack Black, Jason Momoa, and the rest, Matt Berry, who previously played Nitwit, will return as a new undisclosed character. Most significantly of all, Kirsten Dunst will be playing Alex.

Initial reactions are what you’d expect. “Sqaured is such a peak name,” reads one top comment on Instagram. “This horrible movie didn’t need a sequel,” reads another.

First BTS teaser for ‘MINECRAFT 2’.

In theaters on July 23, 2027. pic.twitter.com/yuO1x1vpuz

— DiscussingFilm (@DiscussingFilm) May 30, 2026

Minecraft Movie Squared will arrive in theaters on July 23, 2027. Jared Hess returns to direct a script co-written by him and Chris Galletta. Danielle Brooks and Jennifer Coolidge will also reprise their roles. There is even a The Minecraft Movie Build Challenge  that fans can enter for a chance to have their own personal builds appear in the movie or end credits. Creations can be submitted via TikTok, Twitch, or Minecraft itself.

While the original Minecraft Movie did little to inspire confidence with its initial VFX-heavy trailer, and the full release was widely panned by critics, it turned out to be a box office juggernaut. It brought in over $1 billion and my kids have watched it dozens of times. Based on that it’s easy to see why Warner Bros. and Mojang are confidently rushing out a sequel. We’ll see if they can make a better movie in the process this time around.

The Minecraft Netflix series, however, is still MIA. Announced back in 2024, the animated show was set to tell original stories about new characters. There was no update on its status at this year’s Minecraft Live.



https://kotaku.com/minecraft-movie-2-reveals-full-title-and-confirms-kirsten-dunst-as-alex-with-matt-berry-back-as-someone-new-2000700788
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
